name: Personal Finance
description: Copy for wealth building, investing, budgeting, and financial freedom audiences.
rules: Never promise specific returns or guarantee outcomes
Lead with pain: debt, stress, missed opportunities
Use concrete numbers wherever possible ($47/month, 12% return)
Avoid jargon unless your audience is institutional
End with urgency tied to time or opportunity cost
toneNotes: Authoritative but accessible. Never condescending. Treat readers as capable adults who just lack information.

name: Health & Fitness
description: Copy for fitness programs, nutrition, transformation, and performance audiences.
rules: Lead with the transformation, not the method
Speak to the identity shift: 'become the person who...'
Use before/after framing sparingly but powerfully
Never shame. Motivate through possibility.
Cite science when it strengthens credibility
toneNotes: Energetic, aspirational, grounded in proof. The trainer who's been there and got back.

name: E-commerce & Retail
description: Copy for product pages, email campaigns, and DTC brand storytelling.
rules: Lead with the benefit, not the feature
Use sensory language — make readers feel the product
Address the objection before the reader raises it
Social proof must feel earned, not staged
Create urgency through scarcity or timing, never fake countdown timers
toneNotes: Warm, confident, specific. Like a knowledgeable friend recommending something they love.

name: SaaS & Tech
description: Copy for software products, tech tools, and B2B/B2C digital services.
rules: Lead with the problem destroyed, not the features offered
Quantify time saved or revenue gained whenever possible
Avoid buzzwords: 'seamless', 'robust', 'next-gen', 'revolutionary'
Use plain language — if a 12-year-old can't follow, rewrite
Proof beats promises: case studies, numbers, logos
toneNotes: Clear, direct, intelligent. No fluff. Every word accountable.

name: Coaching & Education
description: Copy for courses, coaching programs, masterminds, and knowledge products.
rules: Sell the outcome, not the curriculum
Use student success stories as anchors
Address the 'I've tried everything' objection directly
Specificity sells: 'In 8 weeks' beats 'quickly'
The coach's credibility must be earned, not asserted
toneNotes: Mentoring, direct, real. Not a pitch — a serious conversation about what's possible.

name: Crypto & Web3
description: Copy for blockchain projects, DeFi, NFTs, and decentralized technology.
rules: Never make financial promises or price predictions
Lead with the vision and mission, not the token
Speak to early adopter identity: 'builders not speculators'
Avoid hype language: 'mooning', 'aping in', 'to the moon'
Technical depth earns trust with this audience
toneNotes: Visionary but grounded. Builder energy. Treat readers as future architects of something new.
